title: Untitled document
text_content:
That means the ln command probably failed, but the script still printed “Created” because the echo ran anyway.
Run this exact version. It uses set -e so it will stop if anything fails:
cd "/mnt/c/Users/antonys beast pc/dev/OSv2"

set -e

echo "PWD is: $PWD"
test -d "$PWD" && echo "Repo path exists"

sudo mkdir -p /opt
sudo rm -rf /opt/OS
sudo ln -s "$PWD" /opt/OS

echo "--- /opt listing ---"
ls -la /opt

echo "--- /opt/OS symlink ---"
ls -ld /opt/OS

echo "--- resolved target ---"
readlink /opt/OS

echo "--- target contents ---"
ls -la /opt/OS | head
You want to see something like:
/opt/OS -> /mnt/c/Users/antonys beast pc/dev/OSv2
Then retry:
cat ~/eos_inbox/umh_core.txt | claude
If it still says /opt/OS does not exist, run this fallback instead:
cd "/mnt/c/Users/antonys beast pc/dev/OSv2"

sudo rm -rf /opt/OS
sudo mkdir -p /opt/OS
sudo mount --bind "$PWD" /opt/OS

ls -la /opt/OS | head
Then retry Claude again.





cat > ~/.tmux.conf <<'EOF'
# --- TMUX USABILITY SETUP ---

# Enable mouse scrolling, pane selection, resizing
set -g mouse on

# Bigger scrollback history
set -g history-limit 100000

# Use vi-style copy mode
setw -g mode-keys vi

# Start windows/panes at 1 instead of 0
set -g base-index 1
setw -g pane-base-index 1

# More responsive escape timing
set -sg escape-time 10

# Easier reload
bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display-message "tmux config reloaded"

# Enter copy mode with Prefix + [
bind [ copy-mode

# Vi copy-mode bindings:
# v = start selection
# y = copy selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-selection-and-cancel

# Also allow Enter to copy selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-selection-and-cancel

# Make PageUp enter copy mode and scroll up
bind -n PageUp copy-mode -u

# Status bar readability
set -g status-interval 5
set -g status-left-length 40
set -g status-right-length 120
EOF

tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf
echo "tmux scrolling/copy mode enabled."
